author: infamousrev
content: To deal with the quantum threat: Would it be idea for the Ethereum foundation to launch an L2 type network (to prevent it from spamming mainnet) where people can simply indicate intent to upgrade wallet-abc to wallet-hash-xyz (without exposing the public key of wallet-xyz), this allows people with exposed public key who didn't generate their wallet using a seed to prove they owned the wallet retroactively (people who generated from seed can already prove this). The key concept to use technologies that are already time tested and production ready, so it can be rolled out as soon as possible. It wouldn't require waiting before Winternitz signatures/STARKs/account abstraction are in place. It allows people to use existing hardware wallets and existing software to sign the failsafe transaction, and never expose the public key of the failsafe wallet, while allowing continued usage of the existing wallet until Ethereum fundamentally upgrades to quantum safe signatures.

author: infamousrev
content: The L2 would serve more as a logged proof that you had access to the private key at that point in time, and it gives you an additional secret (the private key of the wallet that didnt have its public key exposed) that is quantum safe and can be used to prove your the legitimate owner of the wallet and not someone who used a quantum computer to obtain the privatekey

author: infamousrev
content: It could be explained as a sort of quantum insurance chain, it allows any wallet to sign the transaction of the L2 and have a failsafe in case quantum computers progress much faster than expected necessitating disabling the unsafe signature scheme abruptly.

author: infamousrev
content: It would offer a solution that can be used by people with wallets that cannot be saved with the method vitalik described here (because the private key was generated without any form of hashing): https://ethresear.ch/t/how-to-hard-fork-to-save-most-users-funds-in-a-quantum-emergency/18901
